audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra purification/sin offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for unintentional sin/misalignment, priest, congregation, leader, common person, blood/life manipulation, and forgiveness.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel, saying: When a soul/life sins/misaligns in error/unintentionally from any commands of YHWH that should not be done, and does one from them,

if anointed priest sins/misaligns, bringing guilt upon the people, then he shall bring near for his sin/misalignment that he sinned a bull, son of herd, complete one, to YHWH for sin/misalignment offering.
He shall bring the bull to entrance of tent of meeting before YHWH, lay his hand on head of bull, and slaughter bull before YHWH.
The anointed priest shall take from blood/life of bull and bring it into tent of meeting.
The priest shall dip his finger in blood/life and sprinkle from blood/life seven times before YHWH, before veil of holy place.

The priest shall put from blood/life on horns of altar of fragrant incense before YHWH in tent of meeting, and all blood/life of bull he shall pour at base of altar of burnt offering at entrance of tent of meeting.
All fat of bull of sin/misalignment offering he shall lift from it: fat covering inward parts and all fat on inward parts,
two kidneys and fat on them by loins, and lobe on liver; with kidneys he shall remove it,
as it is lifted from ox of sacrifice of peace/well-being; priest shall turn them into smoke on altar of burnt offering.
But skin of bull and all its flesh, with its head, legs, inward parts, and dung,
all the bull he shall bring out to outside of camp, to clean place, to ash-pour, and burn it on wood with fire; upon ash-pour it shall be burned.
If all congregation of Israel errs, and matter is hidden from eyes of assembly, and they do one from all commands of YHWH that should not be done, and become guilty,
when sin/misalignment becomes known that they sinned, assembly shall bring near bull, son of herd, for sin/misalignment offering, and bring it before tent of meeting.
Elders of congregation shall lay their hands on head of bull before YHWH, and bull shall be slaughtered before YHWH.
Anointed priest shall bring from blood/life of bull into tent of meeting.
The priest shall dip his finger from blood/life and sprinkle seven times before YHWH, before veil.
He shall put from blood/life on horns of altar before YHWH, which is in tent of meeting, and all blood/life pour at base of altar of burnt offering at entrance of tent of meeting.
All its fat he shall lift from it and turn into smoke on altar.
He shall do to bull as he did to bull of sin/misalignment offering; thus he shall do to it. Priest shall make atonement/covering for them, and it shall be forgiven them.
He shall bring bull outside camp and burn it as he burned first bull; it is sin/misalignment offering for assembly.
When a leader sins/misaligns and does one from all commands of YHWH his Divine that should not be done, in error/unintentionally, and becomes guilty,
or his sin/misalignment that he sinned is made known to him, he shall bring as his offering a male goat, complete one.
He shall lay his hand on head of goat and slaughter it in place where burnt offering is slaughtered before YHWH; it is sin/misalignment offering.
The priest shall take from blood/life of sin/misalignment offering with his finger and put on horns of altar of burnt offering, and its blood/life he shall pour at base of altar of burnt offering.
All its fat he shall turn into smoke on altar, like fat of sacrifice of peace/well-being. Priest shall make atonement/covering for him from his sin/misalignment, and it shall be forgiven him.
If one soul/life from people of land sins/misaligns in error/unintentionally by doing one from commands of YHWH that should not be done, and becomes guilty,
or his sin/misalignment that he sinned is made known to him, he shall bring his offering, female goat, complete one, for his sin/misalignment that he sinned.
He shall lay his hand on head of sin/misalignment offering and slaughter sin/misalignment offering in place of burnt offering.
The priest shall take from its blood/life with his finger and put on horns of altar of burnt offering, and all its blood/life pour at base of altar.

All its fat he shall remove, as fat is removed from sacrifice of peace/well-being, and priest shall turn it into smoke on altar as soothing scent to YHWH. Priest shall make atonement/covering for him, and it shall be forgiven him.
If he brings lamb as his offering for sin/misalignment offering, a complete female he shall bring.
He shall lay his hand on head of sin/misalignment offering and slaughter it for sin/misalignment offering in place where burnt offering is slaughtered.
The priest shall take from blood/life of sin/misalignment offering with his finger and put on horns of altar of burnt offering, and all its blood/life pour at base of altar.

All its fat he shall remove, as fat of lamb is removed from sacrifice of peace/well-being, and priest shall turn them into smoke on altar upon fire-offerings of YHWH. Priest shall make atonement/covering for him over his sin/misalignment that he sinned, and it shall be forgiven him.

translation_notes: This chapter focuses on unintentional/error sin, not deliberate defiance; avoid shame-overreach.
Chatat is often 'sin offering' but also functions as purification offering; flag for lexical audit.
Different social locations are addressed: priest, whole congregation, leader, common person.
Forgiveness formula is explicit: atonement/covering is made, and it shall be forgiven.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 4 to create obsessive guilt. The chapter addresses hidden/unintentional misalignment that becomes known and gives a ritual path for repair and forgiveness.
